title: [BUG] Image now showing on macos preview window or WeChat iOS app
author: samchen945
body: ## 🐛 Bug Report Lib version: 4.4.0 ## Steps To Reproduce step 1: Use basic code to add an image to the sheet and save to a file: ```javascript const imageId = workbook.addImage({ filename: '1.jpg', extension: 'jpeg', }); worksheet.addImage(imageId, 'A2:B2'); ``` step 2: On MacOS (15.4.1), click on the saved file, or tap space bar on the file, the preview window doesn't show the image: step 3: Send the xlsx file to a friend on WeChat, tap to view the sent file on WeChat iOS app, the image is now shown too: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/39c4ff67-9549-49f9-95f9-24be84d321d9) step 4: Open the xlsx with WPS, and save it again without any changes. repeat step 2 or step 3, the image is shown in preview window. ## The expected behaviour: Image show be visible. ## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful): None

comments.author: samchen945
comments.body: Unzipped the drawing1.xml, and it seems that `a:ext cx/cy` always 0 comparing to the WPS resaved tags. ``` ----> always 0 ```

comments.author: miahks1287
comments.body:
We are experiencing the same issue with iOS 26 (iPadOS/iOS Files app).

  **Environment:**
  - ExcelJS version: 4.3.0 and 4.4.0
  - Issue: Images added via `worksheet.addImage()` are not displayed on iOS 26
  - The generated `xl/drawings/drawing1.xml` contains `<a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/>` instead of actual dimensions

  **Workaround tested:**
  - Windows Excel displays correctly (lenient parser)
  - iOS 26 strictly validates and rejects the file

  This is a critical issue for our enterprise application. Any timeline for a fix?
